methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $299M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Electra is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Sandeep Nijhawan and Quoc Pham that has raised $299M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the Climate & Energy sector at similar development stages, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $299M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $568M. The Peer-Median method establishes a median valuation of $355M across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. This wide spread reflects heterogeneity within the climate-tech cohort; Commonwealth Fusion Systems' $5.0B valuation represents an outlier, while the tighter cluster around $300–$500M better characterizes the core peer group.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $302M (low), $462M (median), and $653M (high). The $302M–$653M band reflects meaningful divergence between peer-based and stage-progression anchors, with key uncertainties centered on whether Electra's technology maturity and addressable market scope align more closely with the median peer cohort or warrant a premium step-up trajectory. Capital efficiency and go-to-market traction relative to named peers would be critical to resolving this spread.
